message:
refactor(runtime): move per-session auth state from AuthState to ClientSession

Split authentication tracking so that server-wide ACL configuration
(passwords, user accounts, requirepass) lives in ServerState while
per-connection state (authenticated_user, selected_db, resp_protocol_version)
lives in ClientSession. This is the prerequisite for correct multi-client
behavior where each connection has independent auth state.

Key changes:
- Remove authenticated_user field from AuthState (now on ClientSession)
- Move is_authenticated(), requires_auth(), current_user_name() to ClientSession
- Add refresh_authentication_for_server() for session auth sync after config changes
- Add reset_connection_state() for CLIENT RESET support
- Move auth_state from Runtime to ServerState (server-scoped, not per-session)
- Add new_for_server() constructor to auto-authenticate sessions when no password required
- Add can_authenticate_as() helper on AuthState for session validation
- Remove set_requirepass_with_session_policy() (session policy now handled by ClientSession)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
